source.openapi: swagger.json (OpenAPI 3.0.4) reconciled with current controller source on 2026-03-25

endpoints.summary: Self-register a new end-user account and send email confirmation.

responses.notes: Creates a user, assigns the default User role, creates a profile row, and sends a confirmation email.
Validation failure, duplicate email, or identity password policy failure.

success.message: Registration succeeded. Check email to confirm the account.

endpoints.summary: Authenticate by email and password, then return an access token and set the refresh-token cookie.

responses.notes: JSON body contains accessToken, tokenType, expiresIn, and user. Refresh token is stored only in the HttpOnly ca_refresh_token cookie because RefreshToken is marked JsonIgnore in source.
Unconfirmed email, locked account, or invalid payload.

endpoints.summary: Rotate the refresh token and issue a new access token. Refresh token can come from the request body or HttpOnly cookie.

header.name: Cookie
header.description: Optional ca_refresh_token cookie fallback when request body is empty.

responses.notes: Returns a new access token and overwrites the refresh-token cookie.
Missing refresh token or invalid request payload.
Refresh token invalid, expired, or user no longer exists.

error.error: Refresh token is invalid or expired.

endpoints.summary: Revoke the active refresh token, blacklist the current access token, and clear the refresh-token cookie.

responses.notes: Refresh token revoked and access token blacklisted.

endpoints.summary: Get the authenticated user's lightweight identity and role summary.

responses.notes: Includes profile-derived displayName/avatar/timezone plus role names.
No authenticated principal or user no longer exists.

endpoints.summary: Get the authenticated user's full profile model.

responses.notes: Current checked-in swagger does not expose this route; source controller is authoritative.

endpoints.summary: Update the authenticated user's editable profile fields.

responses.notes: Only displayName and timezone are currently bindable over JSON because UserName, Email, and PhoneNumber are marked JsonIgnore in source.
Validation failure or duplicate identity data if server-side source is changed later to bind more fields.

endpoints.summary: Upload an avatar image for the authenticated user.

fieldRules.pattern: server validates magic bytes for jpeg|png|gif|webp

responses.notes: Stores the file through IFileStorageManager and replaces the previous avatar if one exists.
Missing file, unsupported file type, or file exceeds 2 MB hard limit.

error.error: Unsupported file format. Accepted: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP.

endpoints.summary: Request a password-reset email. Response is intentionally non-enumerating.

responses.notes: Always returns success-style text whether or not the email exists.

success.message: If the account exists, a reset email has been sent.

email: The Email field is not a valid e-mail address.

endpoints.summary: Reset password with an emailed token.

responses.notes: Password changed successfully and confirmation email is sent.
Invalid token, unknown email, or password-policy failure.

endpoints.summary: Change the authenticated user's password and force re-login.

responses.notes: Refresh tokens are revoked and the current access token is blacklisted.
Current password incorrect or new password fails identity validation.

success.message: Password changed successfully. Please sign in again.

endpoints.summary: Confirm a pending email address with token and email.

responses.notes: Returns success if confirmation succeeds or if the email is already confirmed.
Invalid request, invalid token, or unknown email.

endpoints.summary: Resend the email-confirmation message. Response is intentionally non-enumerating.

responses.notes: Always returns success-style text if the account is missing or already confirmed.

success.message: If the account exists and is unconfirmed, a confirmation email has been sent.

email: The Email field is not a valid e-mail address.

endpoints.summary: List users with optional pagination, search, and filters. Declared policy is Permission:GetUsers.

query.name: page
query.description: 1-based page index. If omitted together with pageSize, controller returns all users (legacy behavior).

query.name: pageSize
query.description: Page size when page is provided. Server caps this at 100.

query.name: search
query.description: Searches email, username, and phone number.

responses.notes: Server always sorts by email ascending.

endpoints.summary: Get one user by id. Declared policy is Permission:GetUser.
Create a user from the admin side. Declared policy is Permission:AddUser.

fieldRules.pattern: array of role names; defaults to ['User']

responses.notes: Response shape is { user, roles, emailConfirmationRequired, message }. Checked-in swagger is stale here because it still models roleName instead of roles[].
Unknown role, duplicate email, or identity creation failure.

endpoints.summary: Replace mutable admin-facing user fields. Declared policy is Permission:UpdateUser.

responses.notes: Treat this as a full-object update. Omitted fields become null/default because controller assigns properties directly.

endpoints.summary: Admin-set a user's password. Declared policy is Permission:SetPassword.

fieldRules.pattern: not validated server-side in current controller

responses.notes: Password reset token is generated internally and email notification is sent to the target user.

errors: Passwords must have at least one digit.

endpoints.summary: Delete a user. Declared policy is Permission:DeleteUser.
Admin-send a password-reset email to a user. Declared policy is Permission:SendResetPasswordEmail.

responses.notes: Source route name differs from checked-in swagger, which still shows /passwordresetemail.

endpoints.summary: Admin-send an email-confirmation message to a user. Declared policy is Permission:SendConfirmationEmailAddressEmail.

responses.notes: Source route name differs from checked-in swagger, which still shows /emailaddressconfirmation.

endpoints.summary: Lock or ban a user account. Declared policy is Permission:UpdateUser.

responses.notes: Returns a message and lockoutEnd timestamp.
Invalid lock request or identity failure.

endpoints.summary: Unlock a user account and reset access-failed count. Declared policy is Permission:UpdateUser.

responses.notes: Unlocks account and sends an email notification.

endpoints.summary: List roles. Declared policy is Permission:GetRoles.

responses.notes: Controller returns mapped RoleModel items, not raw entity graphs.

endpoints.summary: Get one role by id. Declared policy is Permission:GetRole.
Create a role. Declared policy is Permission:AddRole.
Update a role name. Declared policy is Permission:UpdateRole.
Delete a role. Declared policy is Permission:DeleteRole.
List roles assigned to a user. Declared policy is Permission:GetUser.

responses.notes: Current checked-in swagger does not expose this route; source controller is authoritative.

endpoints.summary: Assign one role to a user by roleId. Declared policy is Permission:UpdateUser.

responses.notes: Role not found, already assigned, or identity failure.

endpoints.summary: Remove one role from a user. Declared policy is Permission:UpdateUser.

responses.notes: Role not found, user does not have the role, or identity failure.

error.error: User does not have this role.

integrationChecklist: Use source: schema refs as the authority wherever checked-in swagger.json is stale or missing routes.
Do not read refreshToken from login or refresh JSON bodies on success; the live source hides it with JsonIgnore and stores it in the HttpOnly ca_refresh_token cookie.
Treat /api/Auth/refresh-token request body as optional; send the refresh token in the body only when cookie-based refresh is unavailable.

Current source exposes additional routes not present in checked-in swagger.json: /api/Auth/register, /api/Auth/me/profile (GET and PUT), /api/Auth/me/avatar, /api/Users/{id}/roles (GET and POST), /api/Users/{id}/roles/{roleId}, /api/Users/{id}/lock, and /api/Users/{id}/unlock.
Current source renamed admin email routes to /api/Users/{id}/password-reset-email and /api/Users/{id}/email-confirmation; checked-in swagger.json still uses older names.
Current source CreateUserModel uses roles[] while checked-in swagger.json still shows roleName.

Current source UpdateProfileModel only binds displayName and timezone over JSON because userName, email, and phoneNumber are marked JsonIgnore; do not build FE form submission around those hidden fields unless the backend is updated.
GET /api/Users supports page, pageSize, search, role, emailConfirmed, and isLocked. There is no client-controlled sort parameter; server sorts by email ascending.
Treat validation errors as mixed shapes: automatic model validation returns application/problem+json with traceId, while controller-authored failures often return { error } or { errors[] }.
Password-reset and resend-confirmation flows intentionally return success-style 200 responses even when the email does not exist. Do not build FE copy that reveals account existence.

Declared permission policy names exist on admin endpoints, but the current PermissionRequirementHandler succeeds for any authenticated user. FE should still model admin-only UX around the declared policies, but backend enforcement is currently weaker than the naming suggests.
